title: Starvation
headline: The people are starving!
body: Chief, the people are celebrating the new organized life. But we have grown beyond the old wandering stores. The mouths around the fires now ask for something steadier than luck: they demand that we make food.
prompt: Choose how the settlement will answer hunger.

choices.label: We'll feed through fish.
choices.description: Wade the shallows and strike at the fish below. A spear and a riverbank will carry us while the fields are still only an idea.
choices.outcome: The settlement turns to the water. Spear fishers wade the banks to keep the stores from running dry.

viability.warning: No claimed pond or shore looks fishable yet.

objectives.label: Research Spear Fishing
substeps: Open the Town Hall research screen.
Queue and finish Spear Fishing.

objectives.label: Appoint 2 spear fishers
substeps: Open a citizen's Job tab.
Assign or request Spear Fisher.
A spear in the tool slot makes them faster, but they'll fish bare-handed without one.
Mark a waterside storage chest as the drop-off.

objectives.label: Let fishing keep the settlement fed
substeps: Keep spear fishers landing catches into claimed storage.
Your settlement's total food production (the rate shown) must meet or beat what a FULL tribe eats — a whole settlement of mouths, even if a hard season has left you with fewer right now.
Fishing leads here, but every food worker counts; add fishers, claim better fishing spots, or put others on food until it covers a full tribe.

choices.label: We'll feed through fields.
choices.description: Break the soil, save seed, and turn the settlement's hunger into planned harvests.
choices.outcome: The settlement chooses the field. Hunger will be answered by seed, water, and patient labor.

viability.warning: No tillable dirt or grass is claimed.

objectives.label: Research Agricultural Revolution
substeps: Open the Town Hall research screen.
Queue and finish Agricultural Revolution.

objectives.label: Appoint 2 farmers
substeps: Open a citizen's Job tab.
Assign Farmer.
Give access to hoes through the tool slot or tool storage.
Mark a harvest drop-off chest.
Mark a seed source chest.
Bind a field with the Foreman's Rod.

objectives.label: Let your fields keep the settlement fed
substeps: Keep farmers harvesting edible crops into claimed storage.
Your settlement's total food production (the rate shown) must meet or beat what a FULL tribe eats — a whole settlement of mouths, even if a hard season has left you with fewer right now.
Wheat alone won't do it until it's cooked — grow food crops or bake bread.
Fields lead here, but every food worker counts; add farmers or more field tiles until it covers a full tribe.

choices.label: We'll feed through the animals.
choices.description: Pens, rope, and breeding stock can turn wandering beasts into a living reserve.
choices.outcome: The settlement chooses the herd. Animals will be kept, bred, and counted as food for tomorrow.

viability.warning: No claimed grassland is ready for animals.

objectives.label: Research Herding
substeps: Open the Town Hall research screen.
Queue and finish Herding.

objectives.label: Research Animal Husbandry
substeps: Open the Town Hall research screen.
Queue and finish Animal Husbandry.

objectives.label: Appoint 2 herders
substeps: Open a citizen's Job tab.
Assign Herder.
Fence a pen for the animals.
Bind the pen with the Foreman's Rod.
Mark harvest storage if you plan to cull animals.

objectives.label: Let your herds keep the settlement fed
substeps: Breed up a herd and cull surplus animals into claimed storage.
Your settlement's total food production (the rate shown) must meet or beat what a FULL tribe eats — a whole settlement of mouths, even if a hard season has left you with fewer right now.
Herds lead here, but every food worker counts; add animals and pens, cull more often, or put others on food until it covers a full tribe.
